metadata.title: SYNGO_CC_4939
metadata.comment:
Rationale: Figure 6d,g: confocal imaging and double immunostaining showed dystrobrevin beta (DTNB) and Gephyrin (GPHN), an inhibitory postsynapse marker, co-localized in the hippocampal dentate gyrus of mouse brain sections. Authors note that "Dtnb was co-labelled with Gephyrin (Fig. 6g) but not with PSD95 (Fig. 6h), indicating that Dtnb was exclusively present at inhibitory postsynapses."

Furthermore, DTNB was identified as a binding partner of Dp71 (short product of Dystrophin, DMD, which is also shown to be present in inhibitory synapses in this paper) by IP and both proteins seemed to co-localize at dendritic puncta (Fig. 6).

The gephyrin staining had cross reactivity with the nucleus in Figures 3 and 4, on which authors noted: "Arrows indicate strong cross-reactions against nuclear component revealed by anti-Gephyrin antibody which should be ignored". This brings the specificity of the gephyrin antibody into question. Additional annotations of independent experimental evidence would be helpful to support these observations.. Experimental description: From M&M:
Transgenic mousemodel: "Briefly, the crRNA guiding sequence for the intron 62 of dystrophin (Dmd) was designed as follows: 5'-GTGTTCCCTCATGGTTGTAA-3'. (...) For generating HA-tag-insertion mice, the recombinant Cas9 protein (50 ng/mL, New England Biolabs, MA, USA), crRNA (1.22 mol/μL), tracrRNA (1.22 mol/μL), and single strand oligonucleotide (100 ng/μL: 5'-GTCCGCCCCTCACTGCCTGTGAAACCCTTACAACCATGTACCCATACGATGTTCCAGATTACGCTATGAGGGAACACCTCAAAGGGTAAGTGGAT-3') were coinjected into the cytoplasm of pronuclear stage eggs from B6D2F1 mice."

Cultured neurons from transgenic mouse model: "Cultured hippocampal neurons and cultured non-neuronal glial cells were prepared from homozygous HA-Dp71-transgenic mouse embryos (Embryonic day-18) or postnatal day-0 pups as described previously [33]."

Antibody:
Protein, antibody, host, company, dilution
DTNB, 12045-1-AP, Rabbit, Proteintech Japan, 200(IF)
